title: As racks scale, power must change | Wood Mackenzie
gist: As AI racks move toward megawatt-scale densities, power conversion becomes a strategic design decision: cutting AC-to-DC conversion steps reduces energy loss and heat, but conversion efficiency is only one part of a broader infrastructure equation spanning power access, water, cooling, supply chains and grid interaction.

insights.claim: As AI systems scale, the binding infrastructure challenge is shifting from chips, models and software to physical systems: power delivery, cooling, water access, and the speed at which new capacity can be brought online.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: The AI bottleneck is increasingly physical infrastructure, redirecting value toward power, cooling and grid enablers.
insights.locator: As AI systems scale, the infrastructure challenge

insights.claim: As racks move from conventional power densities toward megawatt-scale configurations, every inefficiency in the electrical pathway becomes more consequential, making power conversion a much more important design question.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: Megawatt racks make electrical-path efficiency economically material at scale, elevating power-conversion vendors.
insights.locator: Power conversion is becoming a much more important

insights.claim: For developers, IPPs, utilities, financiers and infrastructure planners, power architecture is becoming a strategic decision much earlier in the project lifecycle rather than a late-stage procurement issue.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: Winners will plan power holistically from the start; treating power as late-stage procurement becomes a competitive liability.
insights.quote: The players most likely to succeed will be the ones that stop treating power as a late-stage procurement issue and instead plan holistically

insights.claim: Conventional AC-heavy electrical setups are under pressure as compute loads become denser, more dynamic and more power-intensive, drawing increased attention to more direct AC-to-DC pathways.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: A shift toward direct AC-to-DC architectures reshapes demand for conversion, protection and higher-voltage DC equipment.
insights.locator: Nick explains why conventional AC-heavy setups

insights.claim: Reducing conversion steps means less energy lost in conversion and less excess heat to manage, with implications for cooling design and a growing role for higher-voltage DC systems, digital twins, monitoring technologies and new protection equipment.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: Fewer conversion steps create adjacent demand for high-voltage DC gear, digital twins and protection equipment.
insights.locator: The episode also examines what that shift means

insights.claim: The shift is not a simple story of DC replacing AC; the more relevant point is that as racks scale, reducing unnecessary conversion steps improves efficiency and system performance in ways that matter economically at very large scale.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: Avoid the simplistic 'DC beats AC' framing; the investable thesis is conversion-step reduction at hyperscale.
insights.quote: this is not a simple story of DC replacing AC
insights.locator: One of the key takeaways is that this is not a simple story

insights.claim: AI facilities may evolve into more grid-aware assets capable of interacting intelligently with the broader energy system rather than functioning simply as passive loads.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: Grid-interactive data centres could become flexible-load assets, opening demand-response and grid-services revenue.
insights.locator: the discussion widens beyond the building itself

insights.claim: Conversion efficiency is only one part of a broader equation, with reliable power access, water availability, cooling strategy, workforce readiness, supply chain bottlenecks, equipment lead times and safety all shaping whether an AI facility can be delivered on time and scaled.
insights.speaker: Sylvia Leyva Martinez
insights.so_what: Delivery risk is multi-dimensional; supply-chain and equipment lead times are as decisive as electrical design.
insights.locator: But the conversation also makes clear
